data.description: The mobile hamburger menu button doesn't currently change the cursor to a pointer on hover the way other links/buttons on the site do, breaking the interactive-affordance pattern. Scope: (1) locate the hamburger menu trigger component (likely in src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx or a MobileMenu component); (2) add cursor-pointer to the button/trigger; (3) audit for any other missing interactive affordances — hover state (color shift / opacity / scale), focus-visible ring for keyboard users, active state; (4) verify the menu items inside the open hamburger panel also have proper cursor + hover treatment, not just the trigger. Mobile-first note: cursor styling is desktop-only behavior (touch devices ignore it), but the rule should still be applied for desktop users hitting the mobile-style menu at narrow widths. Acceptance: hamburger trigger shows cursor:pointer on hover, has visible hover + focus states matching other interactive elements on the site, keyboard accessible. Out of scope: redesigning the menu itself — just the missing interactive affordances.
data.title: Hamburger menu: add cursor:pointer + hover affordance to match other interactive elements

data.from: Hamburger menu: add cursor:pointer + hover affordance to match other interactive elements
data.to: Hamburger menu: alignment, sizing, and interactive affordances (cursor + hover + focus)

data.to:
Combined task covering both interactive affordances and layout for the mobile hamburger menu trigger.

INTERACTIVE AFFORDANCES (original FRAC-60 scope):
1. Add cursor-pointer to the hamburger trigger so the cursor changes on hover, matching other interactive elements.
2. Add a visible hover state (color shift / opacity / scale — match the pattern used elsewhere on the site).
3. Add a focus-visible ring for keyboard users.
4. Add an active state.
5. Verify the menu items INSIDE the open hamburger panel also have proper cursor + hover treatment, not just the trigger button.

ALIGNMENT & SIZING (merged from FRAC-69):
6. The hamburger icon must be vertically centered on the SAME horizontal axis as the 'Fractal Collective' wordmark in the navbar — both share one centered baseline.
7. Increase the hamburger icon size — it currently feels too small relative to the wordmark. Pick a size that feels proportionate AND meets a >= 44px tap target for accessibility.

WHERE: likely src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx or a MobileMenu component — confirm at planning time.

MOBILE-FIRST NOTE: cursor styling is desktop-only behavior (touch devices ignore it), but the rule still applies for desktop users hitting the mobile-style menu at narrow widths. Tap target sizing matters specifically for mobile.

OUT OF SCOPE: redesigning the menu itself — just affordances, alignment, and size.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Hamburger trigger shows cursor:pointer on hover.
- Visible hover state matching the rest of the site's interactive elements.
- Focus-visible ring works for keyboard users.
- Open-panel menu items also have proper cursor + hover treatment.
- Hamburger and 'Fractal Collective' wordmark share the same vertical center at mobile widths.
- Hamburger icon is visibly larger than current and feels proportionate to the wordmark.
- Tap target >= 44px.

data.body: Scope expanded: merged FRAC-69 (hamburger vertical alignment with wordmark + size increase) into this task. New title and description reflect the combined scope.

Review pass. Diff matches plan exactly. All three hamburger buttons (inner-desktop, inner-mobile, home-scrolled) received identical treatment: cursor-pointer, transition-opacity duration-200, hover:opacity-70, active:opacity-90 active:scale-95, focus-visible ring-2 ring-foreground ring-offset-2 ring-offset-background, rounded-md, flex items-center justify-center. Icon size 24 -> 32. Padding p-2 -mr-2 -> p-3 -mr-3 (tap target 56px, exceeds 44px floor and rhymes with menu rows). Accessibility props added: type='button', aria-label toggles Open/Close menu, aria-expanded={mobileMenuOpen}. Panel items gained cursor-pointer, active:bg-foreground/10, focus-visible:ring-2 ring-inset ring-foreground. pnpm typecheck passes. Only Navbar.tsx changed. Acceptance criteria met. Visual validation deferred to human.
